jewelryCalc.errorFetch: Could not fetch the current price. Please try again.
jewelryCalc.disclaimer: Value calculated at market spot price. Actual buyback price at jewelry stores or pawnshops may be 20-40% lower due to commercial margins.

jewelryPage.metaTitle: Gold, Silver & Platinum Jewelry Value Calculator — Current Price by Karat
jewelryPage.metaDesc: Calculate how much your gold, silver or platinum jewelry is worth at today's market price. Enter the grams and karat (9k, 14k, 18k, 22k, 24k) and get the value instantly.
jewelryPage.h1: How Much Is Your Jewelry Worth? Karat Value Calculator
jewelryPage.subtitle: Enter the weight and karat of your gold, silver or platinum jewelry and calculate its value at real-time market price.
jewelryPage.step1Text: Choose whether your jewelry is gold, silver or platinum. You can calculate multiple pieces of different metals separately.
jewelryPage.step2Text: Use a precision scale to weigh the piece. Most kitchen scales measure to one tenth of a gram.
jewelryPage.step3Text: Look for the hallmark stamped on the jewelry: 750 = 18k, 585 = 14k, 925 = sterling silver, 950 = platinum. If unsure, choose 18k for gold.
jewelryPage.step4Text: The calculator multiplies the pure metal weight (grams × purity) by the current spot price fetched in real time.
jewelryPage.faqTitle: Frequently asked questions about gold value
jewelryPage.faq1q: How do I find out the karat of my jewelry?
jewelryPage.faq1a: Look for the hallmark stamped inside the ring band or on the clasp of a necklace. Common marks are 750 (18k), 585 (14k) and 375 (9k). If there's no stamp, a jeweler can test it for you.
jewelryPage.faq2q: Will I get the calculated price when selling?
jewelryPage.faq2a: Not exactly. The calculated price is the intrinsic metal value at the international spot price. Jewelers, pawn shops and gold buying companies typically pay 60-80% of spot, depending on their commercial margin.
jewelryPage.faq3q: Why does gold rise during uncertainty?
jewelryPage.faq3a: Gold acts as a store of value and safe-haven asset. During economic crises, trade wars, or high inflation, investors buy gold as protection, increasing demand and pushing prices up.
jewelryPage.faq4q: How much is 1 gram of 18k gold worth today?
jewelryPage.faq4a: 1 gram of 18k gold is calculated by multiplying the pure gold gram price by 0.75 (75% purity). With gold near $3000/oz, 1 pure gram is worth ~$96, and 1 gram of 18k gold is worth ~$72.
jewelryPage.disclaimer: Prices are indicative and based on the international spot price. This does not constitute a purchase offer or financial advice. Actual resale value depends on local market conditions, piece condition and buyer margins.

legalNotice.identifyingDataContent: In compliance with the duty of information established by Spanish Law 34/2002 on Information Society Services and Electronic Commerce (LSSI-CE), please note that this website, metalorix.com, is owned and managed by its holder (hereinafter "Metalorix").
legalNotice.siteObjectiveContent: Metalorix is an informational platform about precious metals (gold, silver and platinum) offering real-time price data, analysis tools, AI-generated articles and email alerts.
legalNotice.noAdviceP1: The information provided on metalorix.com is purely informational and educational.
legalNotice.noAdviceP1Bold: It does not constitute financial or investment advice, nor a recommendation to buy or sell any asset.
legalNotice.noAdviceP2: Displayed prices may be delayed by up to 15 minutes from the market. Metalorix does not guarantee the accuracy, completeness or timeliness of published data.
legalNotice.noAdviceP3: Before making any investment decision, it is recommended to consult a qualified and duly registered financial advisor.
legalNotice.ipP1: All website content (text, graphics, images, design, source code, logos) is the property of Metalorix or third parties who have authorized its use. They are protected by applicable intellectual and industrial property laws.
legalNotice.ipP2: AI-generated articles are identified as such and are the property of Metalorix.
legalNotice.liabilityP1: Metalorix is not responsible for decisions made based on the information published on this website, nor for any damages arising from its use.
legalNotice.liabilityP2: Metalorix does not guarantee continuous and uninterrupted service availability. Interruptions may occur due to maintenance or technical reasons.
legalNotice.externalLinksContent: This site may contain links to third-party websites. Metalorix is not responsible for the content or privacy practices of those sites.
legalNotice.applicableLawContent: These terms are governed by Spanish law. For any dispute arising from access to or use of this website, the parties submit to the courts of the holder's domicile.

Before making any investment decision, consult a qualified financial advisor. Metalorix accepts no responsibility for investment decisions made based on this information.

goldToday.title: Gold price today

goldToday.whatMoves: What moves the gold price today?
goldToday.inflationData: Inflation data (CPI) in the US, Europe and other major economies

gramPrice.title: Gold price per gram today
gramPrice.subtitle: How much is one gram of gold worth? Price updated in real time in euros and dollars. We also show prices by karat for jewellery.
gramPrice.howCalculated: How is the gold gram price calculated?
gramPrice.howCalculatedP1: Gold is quoted internationally in US dollars per troy ounce. One troy ounce equals 31.1035 grams. To get the gram price, divide the ounce price by 31.1035.
gramPrice.howCalculatedP2: To convert to euros, the prevailing EUR/USD exchange rate is applied. The euro price fluctuates based on both the gold price and the exchange rate.
gramPrice.karatsDesc: The displayed price corresponds to pure 24-karat gold (999 fineness). Jewellery gold typically has lower purity:

ratioPage.title: Gold/Silver Ratio today
ratioPage.subtitle: The gold/silver ratio indicates how many ounces of silver are needed to buy one ounce of gold. It is one of the oldest and most followed metrics among precious metals investors.
ratioPage.whatIsP1: The gold/silver ratio is simply the price of gold divided by the price of silver. If gold is trading at $2,000/oz and silver at $25/oz, the ratio is 80.
ratioPage.whatIsP2: Historically, this ratio has fluctuated between 15 and 120. A high ratio suggests silver is "cheap" relative to gold, while a low ratio indicates silver is "expensive" by comparison.
ratioPage.howToInvest: How to use it for investing?
ratioPage.above80: Silver potentially undervalued. Historically a good time to buy silver or swap gold for silver.
ratioPage.zone6080: Normal zone. No clear signal of overvaluation or undervaluation.
ratioPage.below60: Gold potentially undervalued relative to silver. Consider increasing gold exposure.

roiCalc.profitableP1: Gold has historically been one of the most reliable safe-haven assets. Over the past 20 years, it has risen from around $400/oz to over $2,000/oz, delivering cumulative returns exceeding 400%.
roiCalc.profitableP2: However, past performance does not guarantee future results. Gold does not generate dividends or interest, and its value depends primarily on global risk perception, inflation and central bank monetary policy.
roiCalc.profitableP3: This calculator uses actual historical prices from futures markets to simulate how an investment would have performed in the past.

currencyConverter.title: Gold price in different currencies
currencyConverter.subtitle: Check gold, silver and platinum prices in 11 international currencies. Choose between troy ounce, gram or kilogram. Exchange rates updated in real time.
currencyConverter.whyCurrency: Why does currency matter when investing in gold?
currencyConverter.whyCurrencyP1: Gold is quoted internationally in US dollars (USD), but the real cost of your investment depends on the currency you operate in. An investor outside the US may see gold rising in USD while it remains flat in their local currency if their currency strengthens against the dollar.
currencyConverter.whyCurrencyP2: That's why it's essential to check the price in your local currency. This tool shows you the updated price in EUR, GBP, CHF, JPY, AUD, CAD, CNY, INR, MXN and BRL, per troy ounce, gram and kilogram.

comparatorPage.title: Comparator: Gold vs Silver vs Platinum
comparatorPage.subtitle: Compare the historical performance of the three main precious metals. Toggle each metal on or off, switch between percentage change and absolute price, and select your preferred time period.
comparatorPage.interpretP1: In "% Change" mode, all metals start at 0% at the beginning of the selected period, making it easy to compare which metal has gained or lost more in relative terms.
comparatorPage.interpretP2: In "Price USD" mode, the absolute dollar price per troy ounce is shown. Since gold trades at a much higher price than silver, this mode is useful for tracking each metal's individual trajectory.
comparatorPage.goldFactors: Safe haven, central bank reserves, inflation

calendarPage.title: Economic calendar
calendarPage.subtitle: Key macroeconomic events that directly impact gold, silver and platinum prices. From central bank decisions to inflation data.
calendarPage.noEvents: No upcoming events matching the selected filters.
calendarPage.whyFollowP1: Precious metals are extremely sensitive to monetary policy and macroeconomic data. A surprise central bank decision on interest rates can move gold prices 2-3% in a matter of minutes.
calendarPage.whyFollowP2: Knowing the dates of these events allows you to anticipate periods of high volatility, adjust your positions and make more informed decisions. Events marked as "high" impact are those that have historically generated the largest moves in precious metals.

article.aiDisclaimer: This article was automatically generated by artificial intelligence from public sources (Reuters, Kitco, CNBC, Investing.com, among others). Metalorix is not the original author of the cited data or news. It does not constitute financial advice. Check the original sources linked at the end of the article.

dcaCalculator.title: DCA Calculator
dcaCalculator.description: Simulate periodic investments in precious metals with Dollar Cost Averaging.
dcaCalculator.dcaExplanation: Dollar Cost Averaging means investing a fixed amount periodically, regardless of price. You buy more when the price is low and less when it's high, reducing the impact of volatility. This simulation uses approximate historical data and does not guarantee future results.
dcaCalculator.enterAmount: Enter a monthly amount to simulate.

goldSilverRatio.title: Gold / Silver Ratio
goldSilverRatio.silverExpensiveDesc: Silver is relatively expensive compared to gold. Historically, ratios below 50 suggest silver may be overvalued.
goldSilverRatio.neutralZoneDesc: The ratio is in a neutral range. There is no clear signal of silver being undervalued or overvalued relative to gold.
goldSilverRatio.silverCheapDesc: Silver is relatively cheap compared to gold. Historically, ratios above 70 suggest a potential opportunity in silver.
goldSilverRatio.silverVeryCheapDesc: Silver is highly undervalued relative to gold. Ratios above 90 are historic extremes and tend to revert over the medium term.
goldSilverRatio.errorLoading: Could not load prices to calculate the ratio.
goldSilverRatio.ozEquivalent: 1 oz of gold = {ratio} oz of silver

investmentMethods.physicalDesc: Buy bars, investment coins (Krugerrand, Maple Leaf, Philharmonic) or historic coins and store them in a secure location at your property.
investmentMethods.bankDesc: Store physical gold in a rented safe deposit box at a bank. The bank does not know the contents of the box.
investmentMethods.bankPro3: No counterparty risk on the metal
investmentMethods.bankCon4: Not covered by deposit guarantee fund
investmentMethods.vaultDesc: Services like BullionVault, GoldRepublic or PAMP allow you to buy allocated gold and store it in high-security vaults outside your country of residence.
investmentMethods.etcDesc: Exchange-Traded Commodities such as Xetra-Gold or Invesco Physical Gold ETC are backed 1:1 by gold bars in vaults. Some offer the right to physical delivery.
investmentMethods.etcPro1: Backed by real gold in a vault
investmentMethods.etcPro2: Tradeable on exchange like a stock
investmentMethods.etcCon2: You don't own the gold directly
investmentMethods.etfDesc: Exchange-traded funds that track the price of gold, silver or platinum. Maximum liquidity and minimum cost. Bought and sold like stocks from any broker.
investmentMethods.etfPro3: Easy to integrate into a diversified portfolio
investmentMethods.futuresDesc: Derivative contracts that allow speculating on gold prices with leverage. Only recommended for experienced traders with active risk management.
investmentMethods.futuresCon1: Risk of loss exceeding invested capital
investmentMethods.futuresCon2: Requires active management and advanced knowledge

currencyPrice.title: {metal} Price in {currency}
currencyPrice.description: Real-time {metal} price in {currency} ({code}). Updated quote per ounce, gram and kilogram.
currencyPrice.whyText: If you operate in {currency} ({code}), the real cost of your {metal} investment depends on the exchange rate. Even if {metal} rises in USD, your actual return may vary based on the strength of your local currency.

comparisons.btcDescription: Complete comparison between gold and Bitcoin as investment assets. Volatility, history, regulation and performance.
comparisons.btcIntro: Gold and Bitcoin are the two most debated safe-haven assets of the 21st century. Both share the characteristic of limited supply, but differ radically in history, volatility and institutional adoption.
comparisons.sp500Description: Comparison between gold and the S&P 500 index as long-term investment strategies.
comparisons.sp500Intro: Gold and the S&P 500 represent two opposite investment philosophies: capital preservation vs growth. Historically, gold shines in crises while stocks dominate during expansion.

btc.goldPros: 5,000 years of history as a store of value. Universally accepted. Regulated with deep institutional markets. Low volatility. Proven protection against inflation and crises.
btc.btcPros: Mathematically fixed supply. Globally transferable in minutes. No intermediaries. High upside potential. Growing institutional adoption.
btc.verdict: They are not mutually exclusive. Many investors allocate 5-10% to gold as a hedge and 1-5% to Bitcoin as an asymmetric bet. The proportion depends on your risk tolerance and time horizon.
btc.performanceText: Gold has preserved purchasing power over centuries; Bitcoin shows cycles of strong appreciation followed by sharp drawdowns. Past performance is not indicative of future results—compare like periods and account for fees, taxes and custody.

sp500.goldPros: Protection during recessions and crises. Negative correlation with stocks. No corporate bankruptcy risk. Tangible asset with no counterparty.
sp500.sp500Pros: Higher long-term returns. Generates dividends. Exposure to innovation and economic growth. High liquidity and low costs via ETF.
sp500.verdict: The classic allocation is 60% stocks / 40% bonds, but many advisors recommend replacing part of the bonds with 5-15% gold to improve diversification and reduce overall portfolio volatility.
sp500.performanceText: Over long horizons the S&P 500 has often outpaced gold in nominal returns, but gold has tended to perform better during systemic crises and inflation spikes. Figures are approximate and period-dependent; this is not financial advice.

fearGreed.errorFetch: Could not load the index. Please try again.

fearGreedPage.metaTitle: Fear & Greed Index for Precious Metals — Gold & Silver Sentiment Today
fearGreedPage.metaDesc: The first fear and greed index specifically for precious metals. Measures gold and silver market sentiment in real time with 5 indicators: momentum, volatility, ratio and more.
fearGreedPage.subtitle: Is the gold market driven by fear or greed today? This exclusive index measures investor sentiment in precious metals with 5 real-time technical indicators.
fearGreedPage.methodDesc: The index combines 5 weighted indicators to measure precious metals market sentiment:
fearGreedPage.comp1Desc: Where the current price stands vs the 30-day average. Above average = greed.
fearGreedPage.comp2Desc: How much gold moved in the last 24 hours.
fearGreedPage.comp3Desc: High volatility = uncertainty = fear. Calm market = greed.
fearGreedPage.comp4Desc: High ratio (>80) = investors fleeing to gold safety = fear.
fearGreedPage.comp5Desc: Silver outperforming gold signals risk appetite = greed.
fearGreedPage.descExtremeFear: Investors are in panic mode. Historically a contrarian buy signal.
fearGreedPage.descFear: Market caution. Prices may be under selling pressure.
fearGreedPage.descNeutral: The market is balanced with no clear dominant direction.
fearGreedPage.descGreed: Optimism dominates. Prices tend to rise but overbought risk increases.
fearGreedPage.descExtremeGreed: Market euphoria. Historically may precede a correction.
fearGreedPage.faq1q: What is the fear and greed index for precious metals?
fearGreedPage.faq1a: It helps investors understand whether the gold market is driven by panic (a potential buy opportunity) or euphoria (a signal for caution). It is not an investment recommendation, but a complement to technical and fundamental analysis.
fearGreedPage.faq2q: How often is the index updated?
fearGreedPage.faq2a: The index updates every 15 minutes using real-time gold and silver spot prices and the last 30 days of price history.
fearGreedPage.faq3q: Is this index reliable for investment decisions?
fearGreedPage.faq3a: The index is a sentiment indicator, not a price predictor. It should be used as a complementary tool alongside fundamental analysis, market news and your personal investment horizon.
fearGreedPage.disclaimer: This index is for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial advice or a buy/sell recommendation. Commodity markets are highly volatile. Only invest what you can afford to lose.
